tables.description: This recipient subject phenotype table contains subject ID, year of transplant, recipient information (n=13 variables; Black or African-American, Caucasian/White, Asian, Indian or Alaska Native, native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander, racial category, Hispanic or Latino ethnicity, age at transplant, weight, height, BMI, diabetes, smoke cigarettes), serostatus (n=4 variables; CMV, EBV, Hepatitis B, and Hepatitis C), primary cause of original kidney disease, primary cause of kidney disease, history of prior kidney and prior non-kidney, receive plasmapheresis prior to transplant, panel-reactive antibody (PRA) positive or negative, no circulatory conditions, coronary artery disease (CAD) requiring drug treatment, CAD requiring invasive or surgical procedure, cerebral vascular disease requiring drug treatment, cerebral vascular disease requiring invasive or surgical procedure, congestive heart failure, hypertension, myocardial infarction, stroke, peripheral vascular disease (excluding venous disease), peripheral vascular disease requiring invasive or surgical procedure, hyperlipidemia requiring drug treatment, cancer (n=23 variables; bone marrow, brain, breast, colon, esophagus, gastrointestinal tract, kidney, larynx, liver, lung, lymphoma, nasopharyngeal, oral, osteosarcoma, ovarian, pancreatic, prostate, skin melanoma, skin not melanoma, stomach, thyroid, uterine, and other), post transplant lymphoproliferative disorder, other cancer primary site, donor information (n=11 variables; Black or African-American, Caucasian or White, Asian, Indian or Alaska Native, native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander, racial category, Hispanic or Latino ethnicity, age at transplant, weight, height, and status), if recipient and donor related, recipient and donor relationship, recipient and donor relationship (other specify), if donation after cardiac death, if expanded criteria donor, primary cause of donor death, donor Kidney Donor Profile index (KDPI), deceased donor creatinine, donor serostatus at Tx (n=4 variables; CMV, EBV, Hepatitis B, and Hepatitis C), cross T cell, cross B cell, cross T-cell match or cross B-cell match, number of HLA mismatches, CMV new, transplant center, simultaneous pancreas-kidney Tx, cold ischemia time, cold ischemia time category, pre-emptive transplant, calcineurin inhibitor (CNI) at baseline (TAC, CSA, both, none), antibody induction at transplant, antibody induction at transplant (IL2 is treated as monoclonal), delayed graft function (dialysis in first 14 days post Tx), off steroids at day 14 post Tx, weight (month 1), creatinine (month 3-1, month 3-2, and month 3-3), creatinine reference point at month 3, death, time to death from transplant, death-censored graft failure, time to graft failure from transplant, max follow up time, recipient GWAS PC cluster (n=4 variables; Caucasian, African American, Asian, Hispanic/Native American), and donor GWAS PC cluster (n=4 variables; Caucasian, African American, Asian, and Hispanic/Native American).

variables.name: PRA_posneg
variables.description: PRA: Positive if any are positive, Negative otherwise

variables.name: antibodyind_2
variables.description: Antibody Induction at Transplant (IL2 is treated as Monoclonal)

variables.name: black_gwas
variables.description: Recipient African American GWAS PC Cluster

variables.name: cainv_base
variables.description: CAD requiring invasive or surgical procedure - Baseline

variables.name: cni_base
variables.description: Calcineurin Inhibitor (CNI) at Baseline (Tacrolimus, Cyclosporine, Both, None)

variables.name: crossbcell
variables.description: Most Recent/Sensitive CM: B Cell result

variables.name: crosstandbcell
variables.description: Cross T-cell match or Cross B-cell match

variables.name: crosstcell
variables.description: Most Recent/Sensitive CM: T Cell result

variables.name: cvdrug_base
variables.description: Cerebral vascular disease requiring drug treatment - Baseline

variables.name: cvinv_base
variables.description: Cerebral vascular disease requiring invasive or surgical procedure - Baseline

variables.name: dgf
variables.description: Delayed Graft Function (Dialysis in first 14 days post-tx)

variables.name: donor_black_gwas
variables.description: Donor African American GWAS PC Cluster

variables.name: donor_his_native_gwas
variables.description: Donor Hispanic/Native American GWAS PC Cluster

variables.name: donorhawaiian
variables.description: Donor Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

variables.name: epstein_donor
variables.description: Donor Epstein-Barr Virus Serostatus at Tx

variables.name: hawaiian
variables.description: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

variables.name: hepb_donor
variables.description: Donor Hepatitis B Serostatus at Tx

variables.name: hepc_donor
variables.description: Donor Hepatitis C Serostatus at Tx

variables.name: his_native_gwas
variables.description: Recipient Hispanic/Native American GWAS PC Cluster

variables.name: othcanunk_base
variables.description: Other cancer primary site: unknown - Baseline

variables.name: pcause
variables.description: Primary cause of original kidney disease

variables.name: pvd_base
variables.description: Peripheral vascular disease (excluding venous disease) - Baseline

variables.name: pvdsurg_base
variables.description: Peripheral vascular disease requiring invasive or surgical procedure - Baseline

variables.name: t2graftfail
variables.description: Time to Graft Failure from Transplant

tables.description: This donor subject phenotype table contains subject ID, year of transplant, donor information (n=11 variables; Black or African-American, Caucasian/White, Asian, Indian or Alaska Native, Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander, racial category, Hispanic or Latino ethnicity, age at transplant, weight, height, and status), if recipient and donor are related, recipient and donor relationship, recipient and donor relationship (other specify), if donation after cardiac death, expanded criteria donor, primary cause of donor death, donor serostatus at Tx (n=4 variables; CMV, EBV,  Hepatitis B, and Hepatitis C), and donor GWAS PC Cluster (n=4 variables; Caucasian, African American, Asian, and Hispanic/Native American).

variables.name: donor_black_gwas
variables.description: Donor African American GWAS PC Cluster

variables.name: donor_his_native_gwas
variables.description: Donor Hispanic/Native American GWAS PC Cluster

variables.name: donorhawaiian
variables.description: Donor Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

variables.name: epstein_donor
variables.description: Donor Epstein-Barr Virus Serostatus at Tx

variables.name: hepb_donor
variables.description: Donor Hepatitis B Serostatus at Tx

variables.name: hepc_donor
variables.description: Donor Hepatitis C Serostatus at Tx

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to cyclosporine (CSA) trough, time spline at day 9 post Tx, CSA trough concentration, weight at CSA trough, CSA total daily dose, CSA frequency, dose-normalized CSA trough, ln of dose-normalized CSA trough, medicine use at CSA trough (n=9 variables; steroid, calcium channel blocker, angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitor, antiviral, ketoconazole, itraconazole, fluconazole, voriconazole, and posaconazole), name of steroid used at CSA trough, steroid total daily dose at CSA trough, steroid use during AR treatment, closest Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR) to CSA trough, closest GFR Modification of Diet in Renal Disease (MDRD) to CSA trough, and closest creatinine to CSA trough.

variables.name: CCB_use
variables.description: Calcium Channel Blocker use at Cyclosporine trough

variables.name: closestGFRCG
variables.description: Closest Glomerular Filtration Rate (CG) to Cyclosporine trough

variables.name: closestGFRMDRD
variables.description: Closest Glomerular Filtration Rate (MDRD) to Cyclosporine trough

variables.name: steroid_dose
variables.description: Steroid total daily dose at Cyclosporine trough

variables.name: steroid_name
variables.description: Name of steroid used at Cyclosporine trough

variables.name: steroid_use_AR
variables.description: Steroid use during treatment of Acute Rejection

variables.name: time_spline
variables.description: Time Spline at Day 9 Post Tx

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to Tacrolimus (TAC) trough (from Tx), time Spline at day 9 post Tx, TAC trough concentration, weight at TAC trough, TAC total daily dose, TAC frequency, dose-normalized TAC trough, ln of dose-normalized TAC trough, medicine use at TAC trough (n=9 variables; steroid, calcium channel blocker, ACE inhibitor, antiviral, ketoconazole, itraconazole, fluconazole, voriconazole, and posaconazole), name of steroid used at TAC trough, steroid total daily dose at TAC trough, steroid use during AR treatment, closest Glomerular Filtration Rate (CG) to TAC trough, closest Glomerular Filtration Rate (MDRD) to TAC trough, and closest creatinine to TAC trough.

variables.name: CCB_use
variables.description: Calcium Channel Blocker use at Tacrolimus trough

variables.name: closestGFRCG
variables.description: Closest Glomerular Filtration Rate (CG) to Tacrolimus trough

variables.name: closestGFRMDRD
variables.description: Closest Glomerular Filtration Rate (MDRD) to Tacrolimus trough

variables.name: steroid_dose
variables.description: Steroid total daily dose at Tacrolimus trough

variables.name: steroid_name
variables.description: Name of steroid used at Tacrolimus trough

variables.name: steroid_use_AR
variables.description: Steroid use during treatment of Acute Rejection

variables.name: time_spline
variables.description: Time Spline at Day 9 Post Tx

variables.name: MZ_TWIN_ID
variables.description: Twin ID for monozygotic twins and multiples

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample ID, body site where sample was collected, analyte type, tumor status, and histological type.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

variables.name: T2

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to biopsy (from Tx), biopsy group, and T-score group.
The subject consent file includes subject ID, consent information, and sex.
This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV) infection or censoring, and EBV infection.

variables.name: t2epstein
variables.description: Time to Epstein-Barr Virus infection or censoring (Days Post Tx)

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to 25% decrease in MDRD GFR, all-cause graft failure, or censoring (from month 3), and 25% decrease in MDRD GFR or all-cause graft failure.

variables.name: compgfr25decrease
variables.description: 25% decrease in MDRD Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR) or All-Cause Graft Failure

variables.name: t2gfr25decrease
variables.description: Time to 25% decrease in MDRD Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR), All-Cause Graft Failure, or censoring (from Month 3)

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to Acute Rejection (AR) or censoring (days post Tx), and AR (12 months post Tx).

variables.name: t2ar_12mo

variables.name: t2gfr25decrease
variables.description: Time to 25% decrease in MDRD Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR) or censoring (from Month 3)

variables.description: Time to Cytomegalovirus (CMV) infection or censoring (Days Post Tx)

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to 25% increase in creatinine, all-cause graft failure, or censoring (from month 3), and 25% increase in creatinine or all-cause graft failure.

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to New Onset of Diabetes After transplant (NODAT) or censoring (days post Tx), and NODAT.

variables.name: new_diab
variables.description: New Onset of Diabetes After Transplant (NODAT)

variables.name: t2nodat
variables.description: Time to New Onset of Diabetes After Transplant (NODAT) or censoring (Days Post Tx)

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, time to index biopsy or censoring (from month 3), and index biopsy.

variables.name: t2indexbiop
variables.description: Time to Index Biopsy or censoring (from Month 3)

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
This subject phenotype table contains subject ID and delayed graft function (dialysis in the first week post Tx).

variables.name: dial_first_week
variables.description: Delayed Graft Function (Dialysis in First Week Post-Tx)
